zh.lifeSummary: 當Margery Bacon於大約1336年9月出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普時，她的父親Edmund Bacon48歲，母親Margery Poynings38歲。她於1352年3月12日與William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年6月1日在她的家鄉享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margery Bacon于大约1336年9月出生在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普时，她的父亲Edmund Bacon48岁，母亲Margery Poynings38岁。她于1352年3月12日与William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年6月1日在她的家乡享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margery Bacon was born about September 1336, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Edmund Bacon, was 48 and her mother, Margery Poynings, was 38. She married William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns on 12 March 1352, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 1 June 1399, in her hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margery Bacon est née environ septembre 1336, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Bacon, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Margery Poynings, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns le 12 mars 1352, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1399, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margery Baconが約1336年9月にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペで生まれた時，父Edmund Baconは48歳，母Margery Poyningsは38歳であった。1352年3月12日にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペでWilliam de Moleyns Lord Moleynsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年6月1日に故郷で62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margery Bacon(이)가 대략 1336년 9월 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Bacon(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Poynings(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1352년 3월 12일 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 6월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns em 12 de março de 1352, em Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margery Bacon родилась около сентябрь 1336 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Bacon было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Poynings, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Moleyns Lord Moleyns 12 марта 1352 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 июня 1399 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.
